cast.name: 斯图尔特·博齐 Stuart Burge
杰奎琳·德威特 Jacqueline deWit
布拉姆韦尔·弗莱彻 Bramwell Fletcher
詹姆斯·诺贝尔 James Noble

writer.name: 阿加莎·克里斯蒂 Agatha Christie
弗莱彻.马克尔 Fletcher Markle

this_title: Studio One: They Came to Baghdad
introduction: This 1952 Studio One presentation of They Came to Baghdad—episode 35 of the fourth season of the American television anthology Westinghouse Studio One—is the sole Agatha Christie adaptation ever mounted by the series.  Adapted for television by Theodore Sturgeon from the Christie novel of the same name, the broadcast compresses the book’s intricate espionage plot into a single fifty-minute live transmission.  What remains is the essential spine of the story: Victoria Jones, an impecunious Englishwoman with an appetite for adventure, finds her holiday in the Middle East overturned when a dying British agent expires in her hotel room, thrusting her into a covert struggle surrounding an imminent peace conference.The episode dispenses with much of Christie’s exotic geography and most of her sub-plots, retaining only the core intrigue that brings Victoria, several intelligence operatives, and a clandestine organization determined to wreck the conference converging on Baghdad.  Directed by Paul Nickell and performed live on 12 May 1952, the production is necessarily studio-bound; palm-lined avenues and crowded souks are implied rather than seen, and the large cast is deployed on a handful of rearranged sets.  The result is less a thriller than a brisk chamber piece—an exercise in condensation whose chief pleasures lie in the elegance of the compression and the period frisson of watching a once-temporary television event that, against the odds, survives at all.
foreign_title: Studio One: They Came to Baghdad
